text.title: Tracking Stack, Targets, Alerts, and Review Cadence
text.summary: Data sources, lean tracking stack options, target-setting approach, alert thresholds, and review cadence for operators.

section.title: Lean Tracking Stack

rule.statement: Use the simplest stack that answers your questions: Stripe → Google Sheets → Looker Studio covers 80% of solopreneur needs before a data warehouse is justified.
rule.rationale: Complex analytics stacks require maintenance overhead that consumes time that could be spent on the business. Looker Studio's Stripe connector provides MRR, churn, and cohort data without custom SQL.

section.p: Stack options by scale: smallest — Stripe + Google Sheets + Looker Studio; mid — Stripe + PostHog free tier + Looker Studio; largest pre-warehouse — Stripe + Mixpanel/Amplitude + Metabase.

rule.statement: Set targets with three-level status thresholds: green (within 10% of target), yellow (10-20% below target), red (more than 20% below target); review red metrics in the next daily check-in.
rule.rationale: Binary pass/fail targets produce false urgency and panic. Three-level thresholds match response effort to deviation severity and prevent over-reaction to normal variance.

section.title: Alert Triggers
section.p: Configure alerts for four conditions: revenue drop greater than 20% week-over-week (investigate immediately), churn rate above threshold (review immediately), zero sales for 24+ hours (check payment system health), CAC above target (review ad spend).

section.title: Review Cadence
section.p: Four frequencies: Daily pulse check (2 minutes — revenue and zero-sales alert); weekly performance review (30 minutes — full metrics table, went-well, needs-attention, next-week actions); monthly deep analysis (2 hours — cohort trends, CAC/LTV reconciliation, churn diagnosis); quarterly strategy review (half day — target reset, OKR alignment).
section.reference: Weekly review template with metrics table and action items
